---
bibliography: ../references.bib
---

# Conclusions

The final assessment conclusions for the `r species_label` stock should reflect
the selected `r assessment_year` model set, its diagnostics, and the agreed
uncertainty treatment.

Until the final `r assessment_year` model set is accepted, the
`r previous_assessment_year` conclusions should be used only as comparison
anchors. That assessment concluded that spawning potential declined until
around 2010 and was then more stable, that juvenile fishing mortality was
higher than adult fishing mortality, that median recent depletion was 0.35 for
2018-2021, that no uncertainty-grid model including estimation uncertainty was
below the 20 percent $SB_{F=0}$ limit reference point, that median
$F_{recent}/F_{MSY}$ was 0.59 for 2017-2020, and that the WCPO bigeye stock
was not overfished or undergoing overfishing.

> **TODO:** Add final bullet conclusions on stock trajectory, recent depletion,
> fishing mortality, uncertainty, reference-point status, and management advice.
> Until those values are produced from the final workflow, this draft should be
> treated as a writing scaffold rather than stock-status advice.
